mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months indicates rising inflation expectations, which can lead to higher interest rates and decreased consumer spending, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over the past 3 months signals a tightening labor market, which can lead to higher wages and increased consumer spending, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-unemployment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over the past 6 months indicates rising production levels, which can lead to increased economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-production periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the RSAFS (Real State and Local Government Finance) over the past 3 months, combined with a 0.5% decrease in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over the same period, signals increased government spending and a tightening labor market, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS > 1% increase in 3 months and UNRATE < 0.5% decrease in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-unemployment and high-government-spending periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) over the past 3 months, combined with a 1% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) over the same period, signals a tightening monetary policy and rising inflation expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 0.5% increase in 3 months and CPIAUCSL > 1% increase in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-inflation and high-interest-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decrease in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over the past 6 months, combined with a 2% increase in the Payroll Employment (PAYEMS) over the same period, signals a tightening labor market and increased economic growth, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 1% decrease in 6 months and PAYEMS > 2% increase in 6 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in low-unemployment and high-employment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Housing Starts (HOUST) over the past 6 months signals increased housing demand and economic growth, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-housing-demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
